name: azure-communication-chat-java
description: Build real-time chat applications with Azure Communication Services Chat Java SDK. Use when implementing chat threads, messaging, participants, read receipts, typing notifications, or real-time chat features.
capabilities: Create and manage chat threads with multiple participants
Send, update, and delete text and HTML messages
Add or remove participants from chat threads
Track and send read receipts for messages
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation for Azure Communication Services Chat Java SDK. All static analyzer findings are false positives caused by misinterpreting markdown code blocks as executable code. The file contains only API usage examples with placeholder values, no actual command execution, network calls, or cryptographic operations. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Creating chat features from scratch is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides ready-to-use Azure Communication Services Chat SDK patterns for threads, messages, participants, and read receipts in Java applications.
userTitle: Build Real-Time Chat Apps with Azure Communication Services
limitations: Requires Azure Communication Services resource and valid user access tokens
User tokens expire and require refresh logic implementation
Only supports Azure Communication Services platform, not other chat providers
Does not handle token generation, only consumption of provided tokens

useCases.targetUser: Java backend developers building customer-facing support features
useCases.title: Customer Support Chat Integration
useCases.description: Add real-time chat support to web applications using Azure Communication Services with participant management and message history

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers creating workplace communication tools
useCases.title: Team Collaboration Tool
useCases.description: Implement internal team messaging with thread-based discussions, read receipts, and typing indicators

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers adding social features to applications
useCases.title: Multi-User Application Chat
useCases.description: Enable user-to-user messaging in applications with proper participant controls and message persistence

promptTemplates.title: Basic Chat Thread Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Java chat thread using Azure Communication Services with two participants named Alice and Bob. Include the dependency, client initialization with endpoint and token credential, and thread creation code.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner developer needs starter code for basic chat functionality

promptTemplates.title: Send and Retrieve Messages
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to send a text message and an HTML message to an Azure chat thread, then retrieve all messages with pagination. Include error handling for unauthorized access.

promptTemplates.title: Participant Management System
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate Java code to list all participants in a chat thread, add a new participant with shared history for the last 7 days, and remove a participant by their user ID. Include proper error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building admin controls for chat room management

promptTemplates.title: Complete Chat Feature with Read Receipts
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive chat implementation with message sending, typing notifications, read receipt tracking, and message update/delete operations. Include pagination for large threads and proper exception handling for all HTTP status codes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Production-ready chat feature with full user experience

bestPractices: Implement token refresh logic using CommunicationTokenRefreshOptions since user access tokens expire
Use pagination with maxPageSize for threads with large message histories to improve performance
Set shareHistoryTime when adding participants to control how much historical content they can access
antiPatterns: Do not send read receipts unless the user has actually viewed the message content
Avoid filtering system messages like PARTICIPANT_ADDED from the message stream without explicit user preference
Do not hardcode endpoint URLs or access tokens in source code, use environment variables instead

faq.question: How do I obtain a user access token for Azure Communication Services?
faq.answer: User access tokens are generated server-side using the Azure Communication Services Administration SDK with your connection string. The token should then be securely passed to the client application.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Azure Communication Services free tier?
faq.answer: Yes, the Azure Communication Services Chat SDK works with all service tiers including the free tier. Check Azure pricing for message and participant limits.

faq.question: How long do user access tokens remain valid?
faq.answer: Token expiration is configurable when generated. Implement refresh logic using CommunicationTokenRefreshOptions to automatically obtain new tokens before expiration.

faq.question: Can I add participants to an existing chat thread?
faq.answer: Yes, use the addParticipants method on ChatThreadClient. You can control message history visibility by setting shareHistoryTime on each new participant.

faq.question: How do I handle message edit and delete events?
faq.answer: Check the editedOn and deletedOn properties on ChatMessage objects. These timestamps indicate when a message was modified or removed.

faq.question: Is HTML content sanitized in chat messages?
faq.answer: Azure Communication Services accepts HTML content but does not sanitize it. Implement your own sanitization to prevent XSS attacks if displaying user-generated HTML content.
